title: 7 Best Herbal Teas For Pertussis
intro: Herbal teas for Pertussis are natural remedies used to alleviate the symptoms of pertussis, also known as whooping cough, a highly contagious respiratory illness. These teas are considered effective in treating pertussis due to their antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which help to reduce the severity of coughing fits, soothe the respiratory tract, and promote the removal of mucus. Some examples of herbal teas used to treat pertussis include Echinacea tea, which boosts the immune system, Thyme tea, which has antimicrobial properties to combat bacterial infections, Licorice root tea, which soothes the throat and reduces inflammation, and Mullein tea, which calms the respiratory system and relieves coughing. Additionally, other herbal teas like Ginger tea, which aids digestion and reduces inflammation, Slippery elm tea, which soothes the mucous membranes, and Wild cherry bark tea, which helps to reduce coughing fits, may also be beneficial in managing the symptoms of pertussis.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat pertussis, also known as whooping cough, due to their anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. The herbal preparation helps to treat this ailment by enhancing the body's immune response and reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract, thereby alleviating symptoms such as coughing and wheezing. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea purpurea teas, including alkylamides, glycoproteins, and polyphenols, are responsible for these benefits, which help to modulate the immune system and reduce oxidative stress. Drinking Echinacea purpurea teas may help to provide relief from pertussis symptoms, promote recovery, and reduce the duration of the illness.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink 2-3 cups of the tea per day to help alleviate symptoms of pertussis.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "BMC veterinary research", Echinacea purpurea teas were found to be one of the most promising candidates for diseases of the respiratory tract, including pertussis.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to treat the pertussis ailment, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, such as hives or itching, and interactions with other medications, potentially leading to increased bleeding or reduced blood pressure. To use Echinacea purpurea teas safely, take it in moderation, monitor your body's response, and avoid consuming it with other medications that may have similar effects, such as blood thinners or medications for low blood pressure.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Echinacea purpurea tea 3 times a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried Echinacea per cup of boiling water.
Gradually increase dosage to 2 teaspoons per cup as needed.
Continue using Echinacea tea for 2-3 weeks to help alleviate pertussis.
Consult a healthcare professional before using Echinacea for pertussis treatment.

preparations.herb_desc: Sambucus nigra teas, derived from the elderberry plant, have been traditionally used to treat pertussis, commonly known as whooping cough. The antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce the severity and duration of the symptoms, including coughing fits and fever. The bioactive constituents, such as flavonoids, anthocyanins, and phenolic acids, in Sambucus nigra teas have been found to exhibit immunomodulatory effects, which aid in boosting the immune system to fight off the Bordetella pertussis bacteria responsible for the infection. The benefits of using Sambucus nigra teas to treat pertussis include reduced risk of complications, faster recovery time, and a decrease in the transmission of the disease to others.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Sambucus nigra flowers, or 1 tablespoon of dried flowers.
Combine the Sambucus nigra flowers with 1 cup of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-10 minutes, then strain it into another cup.
Add honey to taste, if desired, and stir well.
Drink 2-3 cups of the tea throughout the day, as needed, for relief from pertussis symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Sambucus nigra teas can be used to treat the pertussis ailment, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, such as hives and itching, as well as gastrointestinal issues like nausea and stomach cramps. When using Sambucus nigra teas to treat pertussis, it is essential to note that it can interact with certain medications and may not be suitable for individuals with severe allergies or weakened immune systems, so it is recommended to use it in moderation and monitor personal health closely.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Sambucus nigra tea for pertussis treatment.
Prepare tea according to doctor's instructions.
Drink 2-3 cups of tea 3-4 times a day.
Maintain the dosage for 2-3 weeks as directed by the doctor.
Monitor symptoms and adjust treatment plan as advised by the doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, also known as ginger tea, have been used to help alleviate symptoms of pertussis, also known as whooping cough. The anti-inflammatory properties of ginger tea, attributed to its bioactive constituents like gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce inflammation and alleviate coughing fits associated with the ailment. By soothing the respiratory tract and reducing inflammation, ginger tea can help to provide relief from the intense coughing and wheezing characteristic of pertussis. The benefits of using ginger tea to treat pertussis include its ability to provide quick relief from symptoms, reduce the severity of coughing fits, and promote overall respiratory health.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Zingiber officinale root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Zingiber officinale root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid and discard the root.
Add honey to taste and mix well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help relieve pertussis symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Pakistan journal of pharmaceutical sciences", Zingiber officinale teas may have potential benefits for pertussis due to its high antioxidant activity, as demonstrated by its significant DPPH and FRAP values, and the presence of bioactive compounds such as total flavonoid, phenolic acids, and polyphenols.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used as a potential remedy for pertussis, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its high ginger content, which can be particularly problematic for individuals with sensitive stomachs. To avoid adverse reactions, it is essential to start with a low dose, gradually increase the amount, and be cautious when consuming Zingiber officinale teas for prolonged periods or in combination with other medications, especially those affecting blood clotting or blood sugar levels.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Zingiber officinale teas for pertussis.
Prepare 2 cups of boiling water and add 2-3 teaspoons of dried ginger.
Drink 1 cup of Zingiber officinale tea 2-3 times a day as directed by doctor.
Take 1 tablespoon of honey in tea to soothe cough and throat.
Follow treatment for 5-7 days or as directed by doctor to see improvement.

preparations.herb_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas have been studied for their potential in treating pertussis, also known as whooping cough, due to their expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents, including eucalyptol and polyphenols, in Eucalyptus globulus teas help to relieve respiratory symptoms and reduce the severity of the illness by thinning mucus and reducing inflammation in the lungs. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of Eucalyptus globulus teas help to combat secondary bacterial infections that may complicate pertussis. Overall, Eucalyptus globulus teas may provide relief from pertussis symptoms and support the body's recovery process by promoting drainage and reducing the risk of complications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Eucalyptus globulus leaves and 2 cups of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes to get a strong tea.
Strain the tea into a cup to remove the leaves. Discard the leaves.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea, if desired, and stir well.
Drink the tea 3-4 times a day, as needed, to help alleviate pertussis symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Eucalyptus globulus teas can be used to treat pertussis, but possible side effects may include digestive issues, allergic reactions, and an increased heart rate due to its essential oils. Precautions should be taken to avoid using Eucalyptus globulus teas in large quantities, especially in children and pregnant women, as it may cause respiratory issues and interact with other medications.
preparation_usage: Consult a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment of pertussis.
Drink 2-3 cups of Eucalyptus globulus tea, 3-4 times a day.
Use 1 tablespoon of dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves per cup of boiling water.
Continue treatment for at least 7-10 days or as directed by a healthcare professional.
Monitor cough and fever symptoms, and adjust treatment plan if necessary.

preparations.herb_desc: Thymus vulgaris teas, also known as thyme tea, have been traditionally used to treat pertussis, a highly contagious respiratory illness. The antimicrobial properties of thymus vulgaris teas help to treat this ailment by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and viruses that cause the infection. Thymus vulgaris teas contain bioactive constituents such as thymol, carvacrol, and rosmarinic acid, which have been shown to exhibit antispasmodic and expectorant properties, helping to relieve coughing and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract. The benefits of using thymus vulgaris teas to treat pertussis include reduced severity of symptoms, shorter duration of illness, and improved overall health outcomes, making it a popular natural remedy for this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers.
Heat 1 cup of water and pour it over the Thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers.
Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Mix 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) and 1 tablespoon of lemon juice into the tea.
Drink 2-3 cups of the Thymus vulgaris tea per day to help alleviate pertussis symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Ceska a Slovenska farmacie : casopis Ceske farmaceuticke spolecnosti a Slovenske farmaceuticke spolecnosti", Thymus vulgaris teas may be used in combination with other plants to help treat respiratory tract diseases, such as pertussis, by having a non-specific immunomodulation effect and antibacterial and antiseptic properties.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Thymus vulgaris teas can be used to treat pertussis, however, its use may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, skin rashes, and gastrointestinal issues like nausea and diarrhea. Precautions when using Thymus vulgaris teas to treat pertussis include avoiding excessive consumption, monitoring for signs of over-sedation, and being aware that prolonged use may lead to interactions with other medications, especially those with sedative or anti-inflammatory properties.
preparation_usage: Consult a healthcare professional for advice on using Thymus vulgaris tea.
Drink 1 cup of Thymus vulgaris tea 3 times a day.
Use 1 tablespoon of dried Thymus vulgaris herb per cup.
Continue using for 7-10 days or as directed by a healthcare professional.
Monitor symptoms and consult a healthcare professional if they worsen.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea angustifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat pertussis, a highly contagious respiratory illness, due to their anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. The bioactive constituents present in Echinacea angustifolia teas, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, help to boost the immune system and reduce the severity of symptoms associated with pertussis. The consumption of Echinacea angustifolia teas has been found to help alleviate coughing fits, fever, and other respiratory symptoms associated with pertussis, allowing the body to recover more efficiently. Regular consumption of Echinacea angustifolia teas has been reported to provide benefits in treating pertussis by enhancing the body's natural defense mechanisms and reducing the duration of the illness.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea angustifolia root and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the dried root in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth.
Add honey to taste, if desired. The recommended dosage is 1/4 cup of tea, 3-4 times a day.
Consult a healthcare professional before using Echinacea angustifolia tea for pertussis treatment. They may recommend a different dosage or preparation method.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea angustifolia teas can be used to potentially alleviate symptoms of pertussis, such as coughing and congestion, but it may also cause side effects like stomach upset, nausea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. When using Echinacea angustifolia teas to treat pertussis, it is essential to drink it in moderation, as excessive consumption can lead to interactions with other medications and exacerbate underlying health conditions, such as high blood pressure and kidney problems.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Echinacea angustifolia tea for pertussis.
Drink 1 cup of Echinacea angustifolia tea, 3 times a day.
Follow dosage: 1 teaspoon of dried root per cup of boiling water.
Continue usage for 2-3 weeks or as directed by your doctor.
Monitor your symptoms and adjust usage under a healthcare professional's guidance.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, derived from the root of the licorice plant, have been traditionally used to treat pertussis, an infectious respiratory disease also known as whooping cough. This herbal preparation exhibits anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to alleviate the severe coughing fits associated with pertussis. The bioactive constituents of Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, including glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, possess immunomodulatory and expectorant properties, facilitating the expulsion of mucus and promoting the recovery of the respiratory system. By utilizing Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, individuals can benefit from a natural and effective treatment option for pertussis, reducing the severity and duration of symptoms.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh or dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root, 1 cup of boiling water, and a strainer.
Measure 2 teaspoons of the root and place it in a cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the root in the cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain it into another cup using a strainer.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day, as needed, to help relieve pertussis symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be used to treat pertussis, but its use may result in side effects such as increased blood pressure, potassium depletion, and water retention due to its licorice-like properties. To use these teas safely, it is essential to drink them in moderation and avoid using them for extended periods, as excessive consumption can lead to more severe side effects like hypokalemia and edema.
preparation_usage: Drink 2-3 cups of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea daily for 2-3 weeks.
Take 1 tablespoon of licorice root extract 2-3 times daily.
Combine tea and extract for enhanced treatment effect.
Monitor symptoms and adjust dosage as needed under medical guidance.
Consult a healthcare professional before using Glycyrrhiza glabra for pertussis treatment.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas, also known as English lavender tea, have been traditionally used to help alleviate symptoms of pertussis, a highly contagious respiratory illness. The antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties of this herbal preparation help to treat pertussis by reducing coughing fits and relieving congestion, making it easier for patients to breathe and recover. Drinking Lavandula angustifolia teas has been found to be beneficial in treating pertussis, as it helps to soothe the throat, reduce coughing, and promote relaxation, making it an effective complementary treatment for this illness.

Measure 1 tablespoon of dried flowers and place them in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be used to treat pertussis, but they may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, stomach upset, and interactions with other medications, particularly those used to treat respiratory issues. To use Lavandula angustifolia teas safely, it is essential to start with a low dose and gradually increase it, avoid taking them with other herbal preparations, and be cautious of their effects on children and people with sensitive stomachs.

The benefits of using Cinchona officinalis teas to treat pertussis include reduced symptoms, faster recovery, and a lower risk of complications, making it a valuable natural remedy for this respiratory illness.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Cinchona officinalis bark and a tea infuser.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the dried Cinchona officinalis bark to the tea infuser and place it in the pot.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then remove the infuser.
Strain the tea into a cup and drink 2-3 times a day as needed.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinchona officinalis teas can be used to treat the pertussis ailment, but they may cause side effects such as dizziness, nausea, and headaches in some individuals due to its alkaloid content, particularly quinine. By consuming Ginkgo biloba teas, individuals can experience relief from pertussis symptoms, such as reduced coughing and congestion, ultimately promoting a faster recovery.

preparations.herb_desc: Hydrastis canadensis teas, also known as goldenseal, have been traditionally used to treat pertussis, a highly contagious respiratory illness. This herbal preparation possesses antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to combat the bacterial infection and reduce the severity of coughing fits. The bioactive constituents of goldenseal, including berberine and hydrastine, have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, thereby helping to alleviate the symptoms of pertussis. The benefits of using Hydrastis canadensis teas to treat pertussis include reduced duration and severity of the illness, as well as a decrease in the transmission of the disease to others.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Hydrastis canadensis roots, 1 quart of water, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Combine the roots and water in a saucepan. Heat the mixture over medium heat until the water starts to boil.
Reduce heat to low and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes. Strain the mixture through the tea infuser or strainer.
Combine the strained liquid with 1 cup of water and add honey or lemon to taste. Discard the solids.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day as needed. Store any leftover tea in the fridge for up to 2 days.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Hydrastis canadensis teas can be used as a potential treatment for pertussis, however, possible side effects may include gastrointestinal irritation, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications. Precautions should be taken when using Hydrastis canadensis teas to treat pertussis, such as starting with low doses, monitoring individual tolerance, and avoiding use in pregnant or breastfeeding women and children.

The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections, thereby alleviating the symptoms of pertussis. The benefits of using Verbascum thapsus teas to treat pertussis include reduced severity of symptoms, improved respiratory function, and a shorter duration of illness, making it a natural and effective alternative to conventional treatments.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Verbascum thapsus flowers and 1 cup of water in a pot.
Bring the water to a boil, then reduce heat and add the dried flowers.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain it into another container.
Add honey to taste, as Verbascum thapsus tea can be bitter.

preparations.preparation_side_effect: Verbascum thapsus teas can be used to potentially treat pertussis, but its use may result in side effects such as gastrointestinal upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications. Precautions should be taken when using Verbascum thapsus teas, including avoiding its use during pregnancy or breastfeeding, and being cautious of its potential impact on blood pressure and electrolyte levels.

preparations.herb_desc: Curcuma longa teas have been studied for their potential in treating pertussis, also known as whooping cough, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents present in this herbal preparation, such as curcumin, have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects that help to alleviate the symptoms of pertussis. Curcuma longa teas may help to treat this ailment by reducing inflammation, modulating the immune response, and inhibiting the growth of bacteria responsible for the infection. The benefits of using Curcuma longa teas to treat pertussis include reduced symptoms, improved respiratory health, and a lower risk of complications associated with the disease.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh turmeric root (Curcuma longa) and 2 cups of water.
Peel and chop the turmeric root into small pieces.
Boil the water in a saucepan, then add the chopped turmeric root.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes, or until the liquid is reduced to 1 cup.
Strain the liquid and let it cool. Drink 2-3 times a day, 1/2 cup at a time, for relief from pertussis symptoms.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea pallida teas have been traditionally used to treat pertussis, an infectious respiratory illness, due to their immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea pallida, including alkylamides, caffeic acid derivatives, and polysaccharides, help to stimulate the immune system and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, thus alleviating the symptoms of pertussis. The anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties of Echinacea pallida teas also help to reduce the severity and duration of the illness, making it a potential natural remedy for pertussis. Drinking Echinacea pallida teas may provide relief from the coughing and respiratory distress associated with pertussis, while also boosting the body's natural defenses against the causative bacteria.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea pallida root and 1 tablespoon of dried Echinacea pallida flowers.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the Echinacea pallida root and flowers to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 1/2 to 1 cup, 2-3 times a day as needed.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Forschende Komplementarmedizin und klassische Naturheilkunde = Research in complementary and natural classical medicine, Echinacea pallida teas for pertussis may contain immunomodulating active components such as cichoric acid and alkamides 8, 9, but their concentration can vary greatly depending on the type of remedy and the part of the plant used.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea pallida teas can be used to treat pertussis, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, digestive issues, and interactions with other medications, particularly those used to treat respiratory conditions. Precautions should be taken to avoid overconsumption, which may lead to headaches, fatigue, or dizziness, and also to consider potential interactions with other herbal preparations.

preparations.herb_desc: Origanum vulgare teas, also known as wild marjoram, have been traditionally used to treat pertussis, a highly contagious respiratory illness. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce the severity and duration of the illness. The bioactive constituents, including rosmarinic acid, carvacrol, and borneol, have been shown to exhibit bronchodilatory and expectorant effects, making it easier to cough up mucus and relieve congestion. The benefits of using Origanum vulgare teas to treat pertussis include reduced symptoms, accelerated recovery, and the prevention of complications such as pneumonia.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Origanum vulgare leaves and flowers.
Heat 2 cups of water in a pot until it boils.
Add 2 tablespoons of the gathered Origanum vulgare leaves and flowers to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid and drink 1/2 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of ethnopharmacology, Origanum vulgare teas have a high antioxidant potential with a Trolox equivalent antioxidant capacity (TEAC) of 5.87+/-0.2mM and total phenolics content equivalent to 1653.61+/-11.52 microM of quercetin.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Origanum vulgare teas can be used to treat pertussis, but using them may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to their high concentration of thymol and carvacrol. Precautions should be taken when using Origanum vulgare teas to treat pertussis, including avoiding it in people with sensitive stomachs, pregnant women, and those with liver or kidney problems, as it may exacerbate these conditions.

preparations.herb_desc: Artemisia absinthium teas, derived from the leaves and flowers of the wormwood plant, have been traditionally used to treat pertussis, a highly contagious respiratory illness characterized by severe coughing fits. The antispasmodic and expectorant properties of this herbal preparation help to treat pertussis by relaxing the airways and promoting the expulsion of mucus, thereby relieving coughing and reducing the risk of complications. The bioactive constituents of Artemisia absinthium, including thujone and camphor, have been shown to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce the severity of the illness and prevent the spread of infection. The benefits of using Artemisia absinthium teas to treat pertussis include reduced coughing severity, improved respiratory function, and a shorter duration of illness.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Artemisia absinthium leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a cup and discard the leaves.
Add honey or sugar to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for cough relief associated with pertussis.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Frontiers in pharmacology, Artemisia absinthium teas showed no specific information regarding its use for pertussis, but it was found to have anti-infective potential against multidrug-resistant ESKAPE pathogens, inhibiting the growth of MDR strains with an IC50 of 256 μg mL-1.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Artemisia absinthium teas can be used as a potential treatment for pertussis, but it may cause side effects such as gastrointestinal issues, headaches, and dizziness due to the high concentration of thujone, a toxic compound found in the plant. To use Artemisia absinthium teas safely, take precautions such as starting with small doses, avoiding prolonged consumption, and being cautious of potential allergic reactions, as excessive or prolonged use can exacerbate the symptoms and worsen the condition.

preparations.herb_desc: Vaccinium macrocarpon teas, also known as cranberry teas, have been studied for their potential to treat pertussis, a highly contagious respiratory illness. This herbal preparation's antimicrobial properties help to combat the Bordetella pertussis bacteria that cause the illness, thereby reducing the severity and duration of symptoms. The bioactive constituents responsible for these properties include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins, which exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities that aid in alleviating respiratory issues. By consuming Vaccinium macrocarpon teas, individuals may experience relief from pertussis symptoms, such as coughing and fever, and reduce the risk of complications associated with the illness.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Vaccinium macrocarpon teas can be used as a potential treatment for pertussis, but it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions should be taken to avoid interactions with other medications and to monitor blood sugar levels, as Vaccinium macrocarpon teas may lower blood sugar levels and worsen conditions such as diabetes.

preparation_study: According to Journal of alternative and complementary medicine (New York, N.Y.), Echinacea purpurea teas for pertussis were not the focus of the study, but Echinacea purpurea may be effective in reducing the occurrence of subsequent upper respiratory tract infections (URIs) in children.
intro_study: According to "European journal of translational myology", teas for pertussis are not mentioned in the study provided, but teas, specifically green tea, have been shown to effectively improve diarrhea and reduce hospital stay in children suffering from viral gastroenteritis.
